heroDescription: Hybrid Web2/Web3 architecture for sovereign enterprise data exchange, aligned with Gaia-X standards and the Pontus-X trust framework.

overview.highLevelDesc: Sovereign data flow between providers and consumers through the Pontus-X Trust Framework
overview.dataSpaceDesc: Conceptual foundations aligned with Gaia-X for data sovereignty and regulatory compliance
overview.pillar1Desc: Use of Decentralized Identifiers (DID) and Verifiable Credentials to authenticate participants (KYB) without relying on a central directory.
overview.pillar2Desc: Data is not stored in a central lake. Exchange is performed peer-to-peer (P2P) through secure gateways that verify access policies before authorizing the transfer.
overview.pillar3Desc: Immutable record of all data transactions via Smart Contracts on the Pontus-X network, ensuring a perfect, non-repudiable audit trail.
overview.pillar4Desc: Standardization of vocabularies and data schemas to ensure shared information is understandable and automatically processable by ecosystem systems.

sovereignData.title: Sovereign Data Architecture
sovereignData.heroText: PROCUREDATA acts as the Data Space Operator and trust orchestrator. True to Gaia-X principles, we DO NOT store transacted data. Information travels encrypted and directly (Peer-to-Peer) from the Provider's infrastructure to the Consumer.
sovereignData.pillar1Desc: Original data (Payloads) remains in the provider's systems and databases. It is only exposed to the ecosystem when a Smart Contract validates the consumer's access permissions.
sovereignData.pillar2Desc: Information transfer is performed through secure gateways (Access Controllers). PROCUREDATA facilitates the communication channel without inspecting or retaining the content of shared assets.
sovereignData.pillar3Desc: Our technical infrastructure exclusively manages the Federated Catalog (descriptions, technical schemas, ODRL rules) and the Clearing House (traceability and audit logs), ensuring absolute privacy by design.
sovereignData.bridgeNote: PROCUREDATA: Validation only, no data storage

security.heroTitle: Security and Privacy: Defense in Depth
security.heroDesc: Unlike centralized platforms, PROCUREDATA distributes trust through the Pontus-X network and Sovereign Digital Identities. Privacy is not just a legal policy, but a technical impossibility of non-consented access.
security.pillar1Desc: Armored access through corporate Wallets and public key cryptography. Mandatory digital signature for non-repudiation.
security.pillar2Desc: Row Level Security (RLS) policies directly in the database engine to ensure each organization only accesses its own information silo.
security.pillar3Desc: Communications secured under TLS 1.3 and symmetric encryption of data at rest, with keys linked to Smart Contract logic.
security.pillar4Desc: Maximum industrial privacy: allows processing algorithms on data at its original location without transferring files, protecting trade secrets.
security.pillar5Desc: Data minimization and right to be forgotten through pointer management, allowing revocation of access to sensitive data without altering the blockchain record.
security.pillar6Desc: Real-time forensic traceability. Every security interaction is immutably recorded in system logs.
security.ipfsDesc: For static files, we guarantee Irreversible Storage Sovereignty through a three-tier architecture:
security.ipfsStep1Desc: Cryptographic hashing (SHA-256) acting as the asset's digital fingerprint.
security.ipfsStep2Desc: Physical and logical separation between corporate identity and technical content.
security.ipfsStep3Desc: Full control through cryptographic keys managed by the data owner.

web3.pontusDesc: Connection to Gaia-X compatible network for decentralized identity and payments
web3.didDesc: Decentralized identity derived from wallet address. Format: did:ethr:pontusx:0x...
web3.euroeDesc: EUR stablecoin for micropayments. Real-time balance query.
web3.notarizationDesc: Transaction hash registered on-chain for immutable audit.
web3.dataLineageDesc: Example of on-chain traceability for a data transaction

flows.stateDesc: Possible states of a data transaction
flows.flowDesc: Sequence diagram of the data lifecycle
flows.step1Desc: The consumer searches the catalog and requests access to a data asset.
flows.step2Desc: The data owner validates the purpose and justification of the request.
flows.step3Desc: The technical custodian enables access and prepares the payload.
flows.step4Desc: Payment processed, data delivered and notarized on blockchain.
flows.registrationDesc: Entity onboarding process with KYB verification

cta.ctaDesc: Check the complete technical documentation or try the demo
